reflection.title: audit-single-cluster-assumptions
reflection.summary: Audited every setup script, Make target, manifest, and doc for single-cluster assumptions in the Vault/VSO demo and wrote a categorized audit at `docs/vso-two-cluster-audit.md`, mapping each finding to the specific downstream task (02–12) responsible for fixing it.
keyLearnings: - The single hard blocker for the migration is `create_vault.sh`'s VSO `VaultConnection.spec.address: http://vault.${NAMESPACE}.svc.cluster.local:8200` — this in-cluster DNS name only resolves inside kind-vault-lab and must become the external address (task 05/06/08).

- Only one Kubernetes auth mount (`auth/kubernetes`) exists today, configured against the Vault cluster's own API server via `$KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST/PORT` inside a `kubectl exec` into the Vault pod. The `vso-demo` role is currently bound to this same-cluster mount — it must move to a new `auth/kubernetes-vso` mount configured against the VSO cluster's API server (task 07). The existing `auth/kubernetes` mount and its `vault-demo`/`otel-vault-metrics` roles must NOT be touched — they're intentionally out of scope (Agent Injector + OTel demo paths stay entirely in the Vault cluster).

- `vso-demo.sh`, `demo.sh`, `presenterm/vso.md`, and `Makefile` targets all issue `kubectl` with zero `--context` flags — every occurrence needs `VAULT_CONTEXT`/`VSO_CONTEXT` split (tasks 03, 09, 10, 12). `demo.sh` (Agent Injector demo) is explicitly out of scope for context-splitting.
- No `make verify-two-cluster` target exists yet; `verify`/`vso-verify` only ever check one context today (task 11 must add the new target).
- README's VSO architecture diagram and `PODMAN_MIGRATION.md` both depict/describe a single cluster and need full rewrites, not incremental edits (task 12).
- `docs/vso-demo-design.md` exists but wasn't audited line-by-line in this task — flagged for task 12 follow-up.
- No `memory` tool was available in this session's toolset; learnings are captured in the audit doc itself and this reflection for downstream tasks to reference.

reflection.title: add-two-cluster-kind-bootstrap
reflection.summary: Added `scripts/create-clusters.sh` (deterministic, idempotent Podman-backed kind two-cluster bootstrap) plus versioned kind config templates for `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab`, reusing task 03's `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` for shared context/port variables, and validated the full flow live (clusters created, reused on rerun, ports and cert SANs correct).
keyLearnings: - Task 03 (`scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`) had already landed in this repo (parallel DAG execution — 03 only depends on 01, not 02) before I started. I refactored `create-clusters.sh` to source it rather than introducing a duplicate `scripts/lib/ports.sh`, reusing its `VAULT_CONTEXT`, `VSO_CONTEXT`, `VAULT_KIND_CLUSTER_NAME`, `VSO_KIND_CLUSTER_NAME`, `TWO_CLUSTER_HOST`, `VAULT_HOST_PORT`, `VSO_API_HOST_PORT`, and `require_commands`. Downstream tasks (04+) should keep sourcing `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` as the single source of truth for these names/ports — I only added `VAULT_NODE_PORT=30820` to it (task 05 will create the actual Vault NodePort Service on this port).

- Kind config templates live at `scripts/kind/*.yaml.tmpl` using literal `${VAR}` placeholders substituted by `sed` in `create-clusters.sh` (no `envsubst` dependency — not guaranteed present on all machines). Edit the templates + `two-cluster-env.sh` together if the port contract changes.

- **Critical environment finding**: `kind get clusters` (and anything using kind's podman provider list path, e.g. `preflight_two_cluster_network` in `two-cluster-env.sh`) is broken with podman client 6.0.0 — `podman ps --format '{{index .Labels "key"}}'` fails because podman 6's ps format renders `.Labels` as a flat comma string, not a map, so `index` fails with `exit status 125`. I upgraded `kind` 0.29.0 → 0.32.0 via brew, which did NOT fix this (it's a podman-side format change, not a kind-version issue). **`create-clusters.sh` therefore does NOT use `kind get clusters` for idempotency** — instead it attempts `kind create cluster` directly and treats kind's own `"node(s) already exist"` stderr message as the reuse signal. Any future script that calls `kind get clusters` on this machine/podman version will also fail; prefer `kubectl config get-contexts <ctx>` or `podman ps --filter label=...` (without `index`) for cluster-existence checks instead.

- Verified live: `kind-vault-lab` maps container NodePort 30820 → host port 8200; `kind-vso-lab`'s API server listens on host port 6444 with `host.containers.internal` (plus `localhost`/`127.0.0.1`) baked into the serving cert's SAN list (confirmed via `openssl s_client`/`x509`). Both `kind-vault-lab` and `kind-vso-lab` contexts exist in kubeconfig; script never changes the caller's current-context.
- Both real kind clusters (`vault-lab`, `vso-lab`) are left running in this environment for downstream tasks (04+) to build on.
filesChanged: scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh (modified — added `VAULT_NODE_PORT` and included it/cluster names in `print_two_cluster_env`)
blockers: and the port/cert-SAN networking contract is verified against the live containers.

reflection.title: add-shared-context-and-preflight-helpers
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`, a shared Bash helper library defining all VAULT_CONTEXT/VSO_CONTEXT-based defaults (contexts, addresses, namespaces, chart versions, resource names) plus reusable preflight functions (`require_commands`, `require_context(s)`, `kubectl_vault`/`kubectl_vso`, `helm_vault`/`helm_vso`, `preflight_two_cluster_network`, `print_two_cluster_env`) for later setup/demo/verify scripts to source.
keyLearnings: - Canonical names/defaults centralized here: `VAULT_CONTEXT=kind-vault-lab`, `VSO_CONTEXT=kind-vso-lab`, `VAULT_KIND_CLUSTER_NAME=vault-lab`, `VSO_KIND_CLUSTER_NAME=vso-lab`, `TWO_CLUSTER_HOST=host.containers.internal`, `VAULT_HOST_PORT=8200` → `VAULT_ADDR=http://host.containers.internal:8200`, `VSO_API_HOST_PORT=6444` → `VSO_API_ADDR=https://host.containers.internal:6444` (matches the plan doc's recommended demo ports).

- Namespaces reused from `create_vault.sh` verbatim: `NAMESPACE=default`, `OBSERVABILITY_NAMESPACE=observability`, `VSO_NAMESPACE=vso-demo`, `VSO_OPERATOR_NAMESPACE=vault-secrets-operator-system`. `VSO_CHART_VERSION=1.4.0` carried over as the existing pinned version; `VAULT_CHART_VERSION` left blank/overrideable since `create_vault.sh` doesn't currently pin one.

- New cross-cluster auth naming: `VSO_AUTH_MOUNT=kubernetes-vso`, `VSO_AUTH_ROLE=vso-demo`, `VAULT_TOKEN_REVIEWER_SA=vault-token-reviewer` — these are the names task 07 (auth mount) and task 06/08 (VSO cluster RBAC) should use so everything stays consistent.

- `context_exists`/`require_context`/`require_contexts` use `kubectl config get-contexts -o name` (never `current-context`) per the acceptance criteria — this is the correctness-independent check other tasks must keep using.

- Wrapper functions `kubectl_vault`/`kubectl_vso`/`helm_vault`/`helm_vso` are the documented pattern downstream scripts (tasks 04, 06, 07, 09, 10, 11) should call instead of raw `kubectl --context ...`/`helm --kube-context ...`.

- `preflight_two_cluster_network` is best-effort/non-fatal for things it can't fully verify from the host (e.g., in-pod reachability of `host.containers.internal`) — full reachability proof is deferred to `make verify-two-cluster` (task 11).

- Tested with a fake `kubectl` shim since this environment has no real kubectl/kind installed; all four required scenarios (both contexts present, missing command, renamed/missing context, same-context-for-both) produce clear, actionable pass/fail output.

reflection.title: refactor-vault-cluster-setup
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh`, a context-aware, idempotent extraction of the Vault-only setup logic from `create_vault.sh` (Helm install, init/unseal, kv-v2 seeding, policies, same-cluster Agent Injector + OTel demo), explicitly excluding all VSO/VSO CRD/cross-cluster auth installation; validated live end-to-end against real Podman-backed `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` clusters.
keyLearnings: - The script sources `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` and uses only `kubectl_vault`/`helm_vault` wrappers (never raw `kubectl`/`helm`) — every operation targets `VAULT_CONTEXT` explicitly, verified to work correctly with current-context set to an unrelated cluster (`kind-tfe`).

- Preserved `KEYS_FILE`/`vault-init-keys.json` behavior verbatim, including the sealed-but-initialized recovery path and the fresh-init path; only updated the recovery hint text to reference `scripts/create-clusters.sh` instead of raw `kind create cluster`.

- Deliberately excludes: VSO Helm install, `vso-demo`/`vault-secrets-operator-system` namespaces, VSO CRDs (`VaultConnection`/`VaultAuth`/`VaultStaticSecret`), `vso-demo-app` pod, and the `vso-demo` auth role/`auth/kubernetes-vso` mount — these are explicitly deferred to later tasks (06/07/08) per `docs/vso-two-cluster-audit.md`. The pre-existing `auth/kubernetes` mount (same-cluster, Agent Injector/OTel) is preserved untouched and must not be confused with the future cross-cluster `auth/kubernetes-vso` mount.

- Live environment already had a `vault-secrets-operator-system`/`vso-demo` namespace+pod present in `kind-vso-lab` from earlier ad-hoc testing (not caused by this task) — confirmed this script never touches `kind-vso-lab` at all.

- Encountered a Podman VM root-disk-full issue (`no space left on device` during image pull) unrelated to this script; resolved via `podman system prune -af` freeing ~13.7GB. Future tasks running on this same environment/VM should watch for recurring low disk space (VM disk is only 12GiB).

- `create_vault.sh` (the original monolithic script) and `Makefile`'s `setup` target were intentionally left unmodified — out of scope for this task; likely task 09's job to rewire `make setup` to call `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh` (and a future `scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`) instead.
blockers: and validated live against both real clusters.

reflection.title: refactor-vso-cluster-setup
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`, a context-aware script that installs the Vault Secrets Operator, creates the `vso-demo` namespace with `vso-demo`/`vault-token-reviewer` service accounts, and grants TokenReview RBAC (`system:auth-delegator`) — all scoped exclusively to `VSO_CONTEXT` (`kind-vso-lab`) via the shared `kubectl_vso`/`helm_vso` wrappers, with no Vault auth config or VSO CRDs applied. Verified live end-to-end against real Podman-backed kind clusters.
keyLearnings: - Followed the `--check-only` pattern established by `scripts/create-clusters.sh` (task 02): validates `require_commands kubectl helm` and `require_context "$VSO_CONTEXT"` before doing any real work, and exits early with `--check-only` for safe testing.
- Only requires `VSO_CONTEXT` to exist (not `require_contexts`, which checks both) — this script's job is VSO-cluster-only setup, so it should not fail if the Vault cluster isn't up yet.

- RBAC choice: bound `vault-token-reviewer` SA to the built-in `system:auth-delegator` ClusterRole (same pattern `create_vault.sh` used for the Vault SA itself) — this grants both TokenReview and SubjectAccessReview, the standard binding for Kubernetes-auth reviewer identities. Binding name: `vault-token-reviewer-auth-delegator`.

- Reused all naming/constants from `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` (task 03): `VSO_OPERATOR_NAMESPACE=vault-secrets-operator-system`, `VSO_NAMESPACE=vso-demo`, `VSO_CHART_VERSION=1.4.0`, `VAULT_TOKEN_REVIEWER_SA=vault-token-reviewer`. No new env vars introduced.

- Deliberately does NOT configure `auth/kubernetes-vso` in Vault or apply `VaultConnection`/`VaultAuth`/`VaultStaticSecret` CRDs — that's explicitly deferred to later tasks (07/08) per the task notes, keeping this script pure cluster/RBAC scaffolding.

- Verified live: script is idempotent (rerun shows `unchanged`/upgrade to revision 2 without errors), never reads or mutates the caller's current kubectl context (tested by running with `current-context=kind-vault-lab` and confirming it stays unchanged and VSO still installs correctly in `kind-vso-lab`), and `kind-vault-lab` has no `vault-secrets-operator-system` or `vso-demo` namespace after the run.

- A sibling script `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh` (untracked, presumably from a parallel task 04/05 run) already exists with matching conventions/comments referencing this script by name — no naming conflicts.

- Test script includes a regression guard: greps the script for any bare `kubectl`/`helm` invocation not going through `kubectl_vso`/`helm_vso` (watch for macOS/BSD grep needing `[[:space:]]` instead of `\s`, and anchoring `^[0-9]+:` after `grep -n` line-number prefixes when filtering comment lines).

reflection.title: expose-vault-cross-cluster
reflection.summary: Implemented Vault cross-cluster exposure for the VSO two-cluster Podman demo: added an additive `vault-external` NodePort Service (port 8200 → nodePort 30820, matching the existing kind `extraPortMappings`) in `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh`, and created a reusable, diagnostic-rich connectivity-check script plus unit tests; validated live end-to-end against real `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` Podman-backed clusters.
keyLearnings: - `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` (from task 03) already pre-declared `VAULT_NODE_PORT=30820`/`VAULT_HOST_PORT=8200`/`VAULT_ADDR=http://host.containers.internal:8200`, and `scripts/kind/vault-lab-config.yaml.tmpl` already had the matching `extraPortMappings` — task 05's job was purely to create the actual NodePort Service using those pre-agreed values, which I did as a new `vault-external` Service (selector `app.kubernetes.io/instance=vault,app.kubernetes.io/name=vault,component=server`, matching the vault-0 pod's real labels). This is additive and does not touch the Helm chart's own `vault`/`vault-internal` ClusterIP Services, so same-cluster consumers (Agent Injector, OTel) are unaffected.

- Discovered tasks 06 and 07 (`scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`, `scripts/configure-vso-kubernetes-auth.sh`) were already committed ahead of 05 by parallel/out-of-order execution — confirmed neither depends on the NodePort Service created here (07 handles the reverse direction: Vault→VSO API server auth via `VSO_API_ADDR`/port 6444), so no conflicts.

- `scripts/check-vault-connectivity.sh` runs a one-shot `kubectl run --rm -i --restart=Never --image=curlimages/curl` pod in `VSO_CONTEXT`, curls `${VAULT_ADDR}/v1/sys/health`, and treats HTTP codes `200|429|472|473|501|503` as connectivity success (Vault's health endpoint intentionally returns non-200 for sealed/standby/uninitialized states — this is a reachability check, not a readiness check). On `HTTP_CODE=000` (curl couldn't connect at all) it prints Podman/kind networking troubleshooting steps (host gateway resolution, extraPortMappings, missing `vault-external` Service, Podman machine not running).

- Verified live: `curl http://localhost:8200/v1/sys/health` from the host returns 200, and `scripts/check-vault-connectivity.sh` run with the real `VAULT_ADDR` returns `OK: reached Vault ... (HTTP 200)` from inside a pod in `kind-vso-lab`; also verified the failure path prints the expected diagnostics when pointed at a bad port.
- Both `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh` and `scripts/check-vault-connectivity.sh` are idempotent and safe to re-run against the already-live demo clusters (confirmed via repeated runs).

- Downstream tasks (VSO CRDs / VaultConnection in task 08) should use `VAULT_ADDR` from `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` as-is — it's already centralized and this task's Service creation makes it actually resolvable.

reflection.title: configure-vso-kubernetes-auth
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/configure-vso-kubernetes-auth.sh`, an idempotent, context-explicit script that enables and configures a dedicated `auth/kubernetes-vso` Vault Kubernetes auth mount validated against the VSO cluster's API server, writes the least-privilege `vso-demo` role, and was validated live end-to-end (auth enable, config, role write, and a real cross-cluster JWT login returning the `mysecret` policy).
keyLearnings: - Reviewer JWT is minted via `kubectl create token vault-token-reviewer -n vso-demo --duration 8760h` (using `kubectl_vso`), not a static Secret-bound SA token — simpler on modern Kubernetes and documented as a demo-only limitation (not auto-refreshed; re-running the script mints a fresh one and rewrites `auth/kubernetes-vso/config`, which is otherwise a safe, idempotent operation).

- The VSO cluster's API server CA is read directly from the local kubeconfig (`kubectl config view --raw` for `VSO_CONTEXT`'s cluster entry, base64-decoded), not from inside a VSO-cluster pod — this works because kind's kubeconfig embeds the same CA that signs the API server cert whose SANs include `TWO_CLUSTER_HOST` (set up in task 02/03). This is the one intentional bare `kubectl` call in the script (context-agnostic by design), everything else goes through `kubectl_vault`/`kubectl_vso`.

- `vault write auth/<mount>/config kubernetes_ca_cert="$MULTILINE_PEM"` works fine passed straight through `kubectl exec ... -- vault write ...` (no `sh -c`) since kubectl exec forwards argv elements as-is without shell re-parsing, so embedded newlines in a quoted bash variable survive intact.

- `auth/kubernetes-vso/role/vso-demo` is bound only to SA `vso-demo` in namespace `vso-demo` (VSO_NAMESPACE) with `policies=mysecret` only (no `default` policy on the role — Vault still adds Vault's own `default` policy automatically at login, which is expected and fine, verified in `token_policies=["default","mysecret"]` from an actual login response).

- Live-verified against real Podman-backed `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` clusters (already set up by tasks 04/06): `vault auth list` shows `kubernetes-vso/` alongside the untouched same-cluster `kubernetes/` mount; a fresh SA token minted from `kind-vso-lab`'s `vso-demo` SA logs in successfully via `auth/kubernetes-vso/login` and receives the `mysecret` policy; re-running the script is fully idempotent (mount stays enabled, config/role rewritten cleanly, login still works afterward).

- Follows the same script conventions as tasks 02/04/06: sources `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`, supports `--check-only`/`-h`/`--help`, uses `require_commands`/`require_contexts`, and ships a matching `scripts/tests/*-validation.sh` unit test (10/10 passing) that also greps for accidental bare `kubectl` calls and any reference to the same-cluster `auth/kubernetes/` mount.
blockers: and validated live end-to-end including a real cross-cluster Vault login.

reflection.title: apply-vso-crds-and-consuming-app
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/apply-vso-demo.sh`, an idempotent, context-explicit script that seeds the Vault baseline secret, applies `VaultConnection`/`VaultAuth`/`VaultStaticSecret` and recreates the plain `vso-demo-app` pod entirely within the VSO cluster, then waits for the native Secret and pod to be ready; validated with a 19-case unit test suite and live end-to-end against real Podman-backed `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` clusters, including secret rotation and idempotent re-run.
keyLearnings: - `VaultConnection.spec.address` is set to `${VAULT_ADDR}` (`http://host.containers.internal:8200`) from `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`; `VaultAuth.spec.mount`/`role` use `${VSO_AUTH_MOUNT}`/`${VSO_AUTH_ROLE}` (`kubernetes-vso`/`vso-demo`) rather than hardcoding, so any future env override flows through automatically.

- The consuming app pod is fully recreated (delete --ignore-not-found --wait=true, then apply) each run rather than just re-applied, matching the pattern used for `vault-demo` in `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh` — this guarantees `envFrom` always captures a fresh Secret value at pod start and makes re-running the script safe/idempotent for demo purposes.

- The script preflights that the `vso-demo` service account already exists in `VSO_NAMESPACE` (created by `scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`) and that a Vault pod exists in `VAULT_CONTEXT`/`NAMESPACE` (created by `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh`) before doing any real work, with actionable error messages pointing at the prerequisite scripts.

- Live-verified against real clusters: CRDs (`vaultconnection`/`vaultauth`/`vaultstaticsecret`) show `HEALTHY=True`/`READY=True`/`SYNCED=True`; `vso-demo-mysecret` native Secret and `vso-demo-app` env both decode to `larry`; zero `vault.hashicorp.com` annotations on the app pod; rotating the value in the Vault cluster (`kv-v2/vault-demo/mysecret` → `larry-rotated-1`) synced into the VSO cluster's native Secret within ~9s (well under the 30s `refreshAfter`); no `vaultconnection` CRD/resource type exists in the Vault cluster (confirms VSO CRDs are VSO-cluster-only by construction, since VSO's CRDs aren't even installed there).

- Test suite mirrors the conventions from tasks 06/07 (`--check-only`, context/command preflight failures, bare-`kubectl`-wrapper regression guard) plus new manifest-content assertions (external address, dedicated mount, single-container/no-annotations app pod) — all 58 tests across the 6 script test files in `scripts/tests/` currently pass.

reflection.title: audit-single-cluster-assumptions
reflection.summary: Audited every setup script, Make target, manifest, and doc for single-cluster assumptions in the Vault/VSO demo and wrote a categorized audit at `docs/vso-two-cluster-audit.md`, mapping each finding to the specific downstream task (02–12) responsible for fixing it.
keyLearnings: - The single hard blocker for the migration is `create_vault.sh`'s VSO `VaultConnection.spec.address: http://vault.${NAMESPACE}.svc.cluster.local:8200` — this in-cluster DNS name only resolves inside kind-vault-lab and must become the external address (task 05/06/08).

- Only one Kubernetes auth mount (`auth/kubernetes`) exists today, configured against the Vault cluster's own API server via `$KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST/PORT` inside a `kubectl exec` into the Vault pod. The `vso-demo` role is currently bound to this same-cluster mount — it must move to a new `auth/kubernetes-vso` mount configured against the VSO cluster's API server (task 07). The existing `auth/kubernetes` mount and its `vault-demo`/`otel-vault-metrics` roles must NOT be touched — they're intentionally out of scope (Agent Injector + OTel demo paths stay entirely in the Vault cluster).

- `vso-demo.sh`, `demo.sh`, `presenterm/vso.md`, and `Makefile` targets all issue `kubectl` with zero `--context` flags — every occurrence needs `VAULT_CONTEXT`/`VSO_CONTEXT` split (tasks 03, 09, 10, 12). `demo.sh` (Agent Injector demo) is explicitly out of scope for context-splitting.
- No `make verify-two-cluster` target exists yet; `verify`/`vso-verify` only ever check one context today (task 11 must add the new target).
- README's VSO architecture diagram and `PODMAN_MIGRATION.md` both depict/describe a single cluster and need full rewrites, not incremental edits (task 12).
- `docs/vso-demo-design.md` exists but wasn't audited line-by-line in this task — flagged for task 12 follow-up.
- No `memory` tool was available in this session's toolset; learnings are captured in the audit doc itself and this reflection for downstream tasks to reference.

reflection.title: add-two-cluster-kind-bootstrap
reflection.summary: Added `scripts/create-clusters.sh` (deterministic, idempotent Podman-backed kind two-cluster bootstrap) plus versioned kind config templates for `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab`, reusing task 03's `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` for shared context/port variables, and validated the full flow live (clusters created, reused on rerun, ports and cert SANs correct).
keyLearnings: - Task 03 (`scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`) had already landed in this repo (parallel DAG execution — 03 only depends on 01, not 02) before I started. I refactored `create-clusters.sh` to source it rather than introducing a duplicate `scripts/lib/ports.sh`, reusing its `VAULT_CONTEXT`, `VSO_CONTEXT`, `VAULT_KIND_CLUSTER_NAME`, `VSO_KIND_CLUSTER_NAME`, `TWO_CLUSTER_HOST`, `VAULT_HOST_PORT`, `VSO_API_HOST_PORT`, and `require_commands`. Downstream tasks (04+) should keep sourcing `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` as the single source of truth for these names/ports — I only added `VAULT_NODE_PORT=30820` to it (task 05 will create the actual Vault NodePort Service on this port).

- Kind config templates live at `scripts/kind/*.yaml.tmpl` using literal `${VAR}` placeholders substituted by `sed` in `create-clusters.sh` (no `envsubst` dependency — not guaranteed present on all machines). Edit the templates + `two-cluster-env.sh` together if the port contract changes.

- **Critical environment finding**: `kind get clusters` (and anything using kind's podman provider list path, e.g. `preflight_two_cluster_network` in `two-cluster-env.sh`) is broken with podman client 6.0.0 — `podman ps --format '{{index .Labels "key"}}'` fails because podman 6's ps format renders `.Labels` as a flat comma string, not a map, so `index` fails with `exit status 125`. I upgraded `kind` 0.29.0 → 0.32.0 via brew, which did NOT fix this (it's a podman-side format change, not a kind-version issue). **`create-clusters.sh` therefore does NOT use `kind get clusters` for idempotency** — instead it attempts `kind create cluster` directly and treats kind's own `"node(s) already exist"` stderr message as the reuse signal. Any future script that calls `kind get clusters` on this machine/podman version will also fail; prefer `kubectl config get-contexts <ctx>` or `podman ps --filter label=...` (without `index`) for cluster-existence checks instead.

- Verified live: `kind-vault-lab` maps container NodePort 30820 → host port 8200; `kind-vso-lab`'s API server listens on host port 6444 with `host.containers.internal` (plus `localhost`/`127.0.0.1`) baked into the serving cert's SAN list (confirmed via `openssl s_client`/`x509`). Both `kind-vault-lab` and `kind-vso-lab` contexts exist in kubeconfig; script never changes the caller's current-context.
- Both real kind clusters (`vault-lab`, `vso-lab`) are left running in this environment for downstream tasks (04+) to build on.
filesChanged: scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh (modified — added `VAULT_NODE_PORT` and included it/cluster names in `print_two_cluster_env`)
blockers: and the port/cert-SAN networking contract is verified against the live containers.

reflection.title: add-shared-context-and-preflight-helpers
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`, a shared Bash helper library defining all VAULT_CONTEXT/VSO_CONTEXT-based defaults (contexts, addresses, namespaces, chart versions, resource names) plus reusable preflight functions (`require_commands`, `require_context(s)`, `kubectl_vault`/`kubectl_vso`, `helm_vault`/`helm_vso`, `preflight_two_cluster_network`, `print_two_cluster_env`) for later setup/demo/verify scripts to source.
keyLearnings: - Canonical names/defaults centralized here: `VAULT_CONTEXT=kind-vault-lab`, `VSO_CONTEXT=kind-vso-lab`, `VAULT_KIND_CLUSTER_NAME=vault-lab`, `VSO_KIND_CLUSTER_NAME=vso-lab`, `TWO_CLUSTER_HOST=host.containers.internal`, `VAULT_HOST_PORT=8200` → `VAULT_ADDR=http://host.containers.internal:8200`, `VSO_API_HOST_PORT=6444` → `VSO_API_ADDR=https://host.containers.internal:6444` (matches the plan doc's recommended demo ports).

- Namespaces reused from `create_vault.sh` verbatim: `NAMESPACE=default`, `OBSERVABILITY_NAMESPACE=observability`, `VSO_NAMESPACE=vso-demo`, `VSO_OPERATOR_NAMESPACE=vault-secrets-operator-system`. `VSO_CHART_VERSION=1.4.0` carried over as the existing pinned version; `VAULT_CHART_VERSION` left blank/overrideable since `create_vault.sh` doesn't currently pin one.

- New cross-cluster auth naming: `VSO_AUTH_MOUNT=kubernetes-vso`, `VSO_AUTH_ROLE=vso-demo`, `VAULT_TOKEN_REVIEWER_SA=vault-token-reviewer` — these are the names task 07 (auth mount) and task 06/08 (VSO cluster RBAC) should use so everything stays consistent.

- `context_exists`/`require_context`/`require_contexts` use `kubectl config get-contexts -o name` (never `current-context`) per the acceptance criteria — this is the correctness-independent check other tasks must keep using.

- Wrapper functions `kubectl_vault`/`kubectl_vso`/`helm_vault`/`helm_vso` are the documented pattern downstream scripts (tasks 04, 06, 07, 09, 10, 11) should call instead of raw `kubectl --context ...`/`helm --kube-context ...`.

- `preflight_two_cluster_network` is best-effort/non-fatal for things it can't fully verify from the host (e.g., in-pod reachability of `host.containers.internal`) — full reachability proof is deferred to `make verify-two-cluster` (task 11).

- Tested with a fake `kubectl` shim since this environment has no real kubectl/kind installed; all four required scenarios (both contexts present, missing command, renamed/missing context, same-context-for-both) produce clear, actionable pass/fail output.

reflection.title: refactor-vault-cluster-setup
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh`, a context-aware, idempotent extraction of the Vault-only setup logic from `create_vault.sh` (Helm install, init/unseal, kv-v2 seeding, policies, same-cluster Agent Injector + OTel demo), explicitly excluding all VSO/VSO CRD/cross-cluster auth installation; validated live end-to-end against real Podman-backed `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` clusters.
keyLearnings: - The script sources `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` and uses only `kubectl_vault`/`helm_vault` wrappers (never raw `kubectl`/`helm`) — every operation targets `VAULT_CONTEXT` explicitly, verified to work correctly with current-context set to an unrelated cluster (`kind-tfe`).

- Preserved `KEYS_FILE`/`vault-init-keys.json` behavior verbatim, including the sealed-but-initialized recovery path and the fresh-init path; only updated the recovery hint text to reference `scripts/create-clusters.sh` instead of raw `kind create cluster`.

- Deliberately excludes: VSO Helm install, `vso-demo`/`vault-secrets-operator-system` namespaces, VSO CRDs (`VaultConnection`/`VaultAuth`/`VaultStaticSecret`), `vso-demo-app` pod, and the `vso-demo` auth role/`auth/kubernetes-vso` mount — these are explicitly deferred to later tasks (06/07/08) per `docs/vso-two-cluster-audit.md`. The pre-existing `auth/kubernetes` mount (same-cluster, Agent Injector/OTel) is preserved untouched and must not be confused with the future cross-cluster `auth/kubernetes-vso` mount.

- Live environment already had a `vault-secrets-operator-system`/`vso-demo` namespace+pod present in `kind-vso-lab` from earlier ad-hoc testing (not caused by this task) — confirmed this script never touches `kind-vso-lab` at all.

- Encountered a Podman VM root-disk-full issue (`no space left on device` during image pull) unrelated to this script; resolved via `podman system prune -af` freeing ~13.7GB. Future tasks running on this same environment/VM should watch for recurring low disk space (VM disk is only 12GiB).

- `create_vault.sh` (the original monolithic script) and `Makefile`'s `setup` target were intentionally left unmodified — out of scope for this task; likely task 09's job to rewire `make setup` to call `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh` (and a future `scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`) instead.
blockers: and validated live against both real clusters.

reflection.title: refactor-vso-cluster-setup
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`, a context-aware script that installs the Vault Secrets Operator, creates the `vso-demo` namespace with `vso-demo`/`vault-token-reviewer` service accounts, and grants TokenReview RBAC (`system:auth-delegator`) — all scoped exclusively to `VSO_CONTEXT` (`kind-vso-lab`) via the shared `kubectl_vso`/`helm_vso` wrappers, with no Vault auth config or VSO CRDs applied. Verified live end-to-end against real Podman-backed kind clusters.
keyLearnings: - Followed the `--check-only` pattern established by `scripts/create-clusters.sh` (task 02): validates `require_commands kubectl helm` and `require_context "$VSO_CONTEXT"` before doing any real work, and exits early with `--check-only` for safe testing.
- Only requires `VSO_CONTEXT` to exist (not `require_contexts`, which checks both) — this script's job is VSO-cluster-only setup, so it should not fail if the Vault cluster isn't up yet.

- RBAC choice: bound `vault-token-reviewer` SA to the built-in `system:auth-delegator` ClusterRole (same pattern `create_vault.sh` used for the Vault SA itself) — this grants both TokenReview and SubjectAccessReview, the standard binding for Kubernetes-auth reviewer identities. Binding name: `vault-token-reviewer-auth-delegator`.

- Reused all naming/constants from `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` (task 03): `VSO_OPERATOR_NAMESPACE=vault-secrets-operator-system`, `VSO_NAMESPACE=vso-demo`, `VSO_CHART_VERSION=1.4.0`, `VAULT_TOKEN_REVIEWER_SA=vault-token-reviewer`. No new env vars introduced.

- Deliberately does NOT configure `auth/kubernetes-vso` in Vault or apply `VaultConnection`/`VaultAuth`/`VaultStaticSecret` CRDs — that's explicitly deferred to later tasks (07/08) per the task notes, keeping this script pure cluster/RBAC scaffolding.

- Verified live: script is idempotent (rerun shows `unchanged`/upgrade to revision 2 without errors), never reads or mutates the caller's current kubectl context (tested by running with `current-context=kind-vault-lab` and confirming it stays unchanged and VSO still installs correctly in `kind-vso-lab`), and `kind-vault-lab` has no `vault-secrets-operator-system` or `vso-demo` namespace after the run.

- A sibling script `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh` (untracked, presumably from a parallel task 04/05 run) already exists with matching conventions/comments referencing this script by name — no naming conflicts.

- Test script includes a regression guard: greps the script for any bare `kubectl`/`helm` invocation not going through `kubectl_vso`/`helm_vso` (watch for macOS/BSD grep needing `[[:space:]]` instead of `\s`, and anchoring `^[0-9]+:` after `grep -n` line-number prefixes when filtering comment lines).

reflection.title: expose-vault-cross-cluster
reflection.summary: Implemented Vault cross-cluster exposure for the VSO two-cluster Podman demo: added an additive `vault-external` NodePort Service (port 8200 → nodePort 30820, matching the existing kind `extraPortMappings`) in `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh`, and created a reusable, diagnostic-rich connectivity-check script plus unit tests; validated live end-to-end against real `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` Podman-backed clusters.
keyLearnings: - `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` (from task 03) already pre-declared `VAULT_NODE_PORT=30820`/`VAULT_HOST_PORT=8200`/`VAULT_ADDR=http://host.containers.internal:8200`, and `scripts/kind/vault-lab-config.yaml.tmpl` already had the matching `extraPortMappings` — task 05's job was purely to create the actual NodePort Service using those pre-agreed values, which I did as a new `vault-external` Service (selector `app.kubernetes.io/instance=vault,app.kubernetes.io/name=vault,component=server`, matching the vault-0 pod's real labels). This is additive and does not touch the Helm chart's own `vault`/`vault-internal` ClusterIP Services, so same-cluster consumers (Agent Injector, OTel) are unaffected.

- Discovered tasks 06 and 07 (`scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`, `scripts/configure-vso-kubernetes-auth.sh`) were already committed ahead of 05 by parallel/out-of-order execution — confirmed neither depends on the NodePort Service created here (07 handles the reverse direction: Vault→VSO API server auth via `VSO_API_ADDR`/port 6444), so no conflicts.

- `scripts/check-vault-connectivity.sh` runs a one-shot `kubectl run --rm -i --restart=Never --image=curlimages/curl` pod in `VSO_CONTEXT`, curls `${VAULT_ADDR}/v1/sys/health`, and treats HTTP codes `200|429|472|473|501|503` as connectivity success (Vault's health endpoint intentionally returns non-200 for sealed/standby/uninitialized states — this is a reachability check, not a readiness check). On `HTTP_CODE=000` (curl couldn't connect at all) it prints Podman/kind networking troubleshooting steps (host gateway resolution, extraPortMappings, missing `vault-external` Service, Podman machine not running).

- Verified live: `curl http://localhost:8200/v1/sys/health` from the host returns 200, and `scripts/check-vault-connectivity.sh` run with the real `VAULT_ADDR` returns `OK: reached Vault ... (HTTP 200)` from inside a pod in `kind-vso-lab`; also verified the failure path prints the expected diagnostics when pointed at a bad port.
- Both `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh` and `scripts/check-vault-connectivity.sh` are idempotent and safe to re-run against the already-live demo clusters (confirmed via repeated runs).

- Downstream tasks (VSO CRDs / VaultConnection in task 08) should use `VAULT_ADDR` from `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` as-is — it's already centralized and this task's Service creation makes it actually resolvable.

reflection.title: configure-vso-kubernetes-auth
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/configure-vso-kubernetes-auth.sh`, an idempotent, context-explicit script that enables and configures a dedicated `auth/kubernetes-vso` Vault Kubernetes auth mount validated against the VSO cluster's API server, writes the least-privilege `vso-demo` role, and was validated live end-to-end (auth enable, config, role write, and a real cross-cluster JWT login returning the `mysecret` policy).
keyLearnings: - Reviewer JWT is minted via `kubectl create token vault-token-reviewer -n vso-demo --duration 8760h` (using `kubectl_vso`), not a static Secret-bound SA token — simpler on modern Kubernetes and documented as a demo-only limitation (not auto-refreshed; re-running the script mints a fresh one and rewrites `auth/kubernetes-vso/config`, which is otherwise a safe, idempotent operation).

- The VSO cluster's API server CA is read directly from the local kubeconfig (`kubectl config view --raw` for `VSO_CONTEXT`'s cluster entry, base64-decoded), not from inside a VSO-cluster pod — this works because kind's kubeconfig embeds the same CA that signs the API server cert whose SANs include `TWO_CLUSTER_HOST` (set up in task 02/03). This is the one intentional bare `kubectl` call in the script (context-agnostic by design), everything else goes through `kubectl_vault`/`kubectl_vso`.

- `vault write auth/<mount>/config kubernetes_ca_cert="$MULTILINE_PEM"` works fine passed straight through `kubectl exec ... -- vault write ...` (no `sh -c`) since kubectl exec forwards argv elements as-is without shell re-parsing, so embedded newlines in a quoted bash variable survive intact.

- `auth/kubernetes-vso/role/vso-demo` is bound only to SA `vso-demo` in namespace `vso-demo` (VSO_NAMESPACE) with `policies=mysecret` only (no `default` policy on the role — Vault still adds Vault's own `default` policy automatically at login, which is expected and fine, verified in `token_policies=["default","mysecret"]` from an actual login response).

- Live-verified against real Podman-backed `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` clusters (already set up by tasks 04/06): `vault auth list` shows `kubernetes-vso/` alongside the untouched same-cluster `kubernetes/` mount; a fresh SA token minted from `kind-vso-lab`'s `vso-demo` SA logs in successfully via `auth/kubernetes-vso/login` and receives the `mysecret` policy; re-running the script is fully idempotent (mount stays enabled, config/role rewritten cleanly, login still works afterward).

- Follows the same script conventions as tasks 02/04/06: sources `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`, supports `--check-only`/`-h`/`--help`, uses `require_commands`/`require_contexts`, and ships a matching `scripts/tests/*-validation.sh` unit test (10/10 passing) that also greps for accidental bare `kubectl` calls and any reference to the same-cluster `auth/kubernetes/` mount.
blockers: and validated live end-to-end including a real cross-cluster Vault login.

reflection.title: apply-vso-crds-and-consuming-app
reflection.summary: Created `scripts/apply-vso-demo.sh`, an idempotent, context-explicit script that seeds the Vault baseline secret, applies `VaultConnection`/`VaultAuth`/`VaultStaticSecret` and recreates the plain `vso-demo-app` pod entirely within the VSO cluster, then waits for the native Secret and pod to be ready; validated with a 19-case unit test suite and live end-to-end against real Podman-backed `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` clusters, including secret rotation and idempotent re-run.
keyLearnings: - `VaultConnection.spec.address` is set to `${VAULT_ADDR}` (`http://host.containers.internal:8200`) from `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`; `VaultAuth.spec.mount`/`role` use `${VSO_AUTH_MOUNT}`/`${VSO_AUTH_ROLE}` (`kubernetes-vso`/`vso-demo`) rather than hardcoding, so any future env override flows through automatically.

- The consuming app pod is fully recreated (delete --ignore-not-found --wait=true, then apply) each run rather than just re-applied, matching the pattern used for `vault-demo` in `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh` — this guarantees `envFrom` always captures a fresh Secret value at pod start and makes re-running the script safe/idempotent for demo purposes.

- The script preflights that the `vso-demo` service account already exists in `VSO_NAMESPACE` (created by `scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`) and that a Vault pod exists in `VAULT_CONTEXT`/`NAMESPACE` (created by `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh`) before doing any real work, with actionable error messages pointing at the prerequisite scripts.

- Live-verified against real clusters: CRDs (`vaultconnection`/`vaultauth`/`vaultstaticsecret`) show `HEALTHY=True`/`READY=True`/`SYNCED=True`; `vso-demo-mysecret` native Secret and `vso-demo-app` env both decode to `larry`; zero `vault.hashicorp.com` annotations on the app pod; rotating the value in the Vault cluster (`kv-v2/vault-demo/mysecret` → `larry-rotated-1`) synced into the VSO cluster's native Secret within ~9s (well under the 30s `refreshAfter`); no `vaultconnection` CRD/resource type exists in the Vault cluster (confirms VSO CRDs are VSO-cluster-only by construction, since VSO's CRDs aren't even installed there).

- Test suite mirrors the conventions from tasks 06/07 (`--check-only`, context/command preflight failures, bare-`kubectl`-wrapper regression guard) plus new manifest-content assertions (external address, dedicated mount, single-container/no-annotations app pod) — all 58 tests across the 6 script test files in `scripts/tests/` currently pass.
